structure.description: Render boolean/enum attributes of mainEntity as compact badge row directly under title in detail hero, before long-form description. Badges carry type modifiers (Remote, Full-time) and urgency signals (Urgently hiring).

rationale.hypothesis: Categorical attributes that determine fit/no-fit decisions compress better as visual chips than as labeled fields. Placing them in hero enables sub-second eligibility scan before the user commits to reading description.

evidence.description: Hero shows '$25/hr · Full-time · Remote · Urgently hiring' as inline badges before description section
Same badge row under title on job detail
Amenity chips (Wifi, Kitchen, Washer) in hero area

counterexample.description: Detail forms with many boolean options use checklists, not hero badges — badges only work when attributes are mutually compatible filters, not config toggles

shouldMatch.reason: Listing has type/condition/shipping as boolean+enum — badge row reinforces scan

shouldNotMatch.reason: Execution attributes are telemetry, not categorical scan signals
MoodEntry fields are not eligibility filters
